tool_args.label: Long Island Rail Road Procurements - July 2023

payload.title: Long Island Rail Road Procurements
payload.description: LIRR Procurement Agenda for MTA Board approval - includes 1 action for a proposed estimated expenditure of $56.2M for Rail Grinding Services

payload.title: MTA Assistant Deputy Chief Procurement Officer – LIRR Procurements

tool_args.label: MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights

payload.entity_name: MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights

payload.description: Comprised of representatives from LIRR, MNR, and NYC Transit Maintenance of Way user groups

payload.project_name: Joint Agency Rail Grinding Services for LIRR/MNR/NYCT
payload.description: Joint Agency (Long Island Rail Road, Metro-North Railroad, and NYC Transit) publicly advertised and competitively solicited Request for Proposal for Rail Grinding Services, to be awarded to Loram Maintenance of Way, Inc.
MTA Procurement Operations published the advertisement for the Rail Grinding Services procurement in the New York State Contract Reporter, New York Daily News, and on the MTA website
Proposal received from Loram for Rail Grinding Services. Four other contractors submitted No Proposal Response Forms.
MTA Selection Committee evaluated Loram's proposal and unanimously determined Loram was technically qualified to perform the work
Long Island Rail Road Committee meeting to review procurement actions
MTA Board meeting to consider procurement resolutions
Five-year estimated total for LIRR, MNR, and NYCT for Rail Grinding Services contract
2-year base amount for Rail Grinding Services contract
Three 1-year options total amount for Rail Grinding Services contract
Agencies combined year-one estimate based on previous program usage
LIRR year-one estimate based on previous program usage

payload.claim_text: The benefits of a rail grinding program include (1) reduction of broken rails; (2) extension of rail life; (3) extended track components life, and (4) improved ride quality and passenger comfort.
payload.description: Benefits of rail grinding program as described in the procurement justification

payload.claim_text: Funding for this project is included in the LIRR and MNR Capital Program Budget. NYC Transit will be utilizing Operating funds for their portion of the contract.
payload.description: Funding source allocation for the Rail Grinding Services contract

payload.claim_text: Rail grinding improves the safety and stability of the rails and reduces the need to perform premature rail replacement.
payload.description: Safety purpose of rail grinding services

tool_args.label: Exploration of Alternatives to Rail Grinding

payload.claim_text: All three agencies have made the decision to explore alternatives to rail grinding. This may include rail milling services, which may be a cost-efficient supplement to rail grinding where practical.
payload.description: Agencies exploring rail milling as potential supplement to rail grinding

payload.claim_text: The MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights ("DDCR") has established a zero percent DBE goal for this contract.
payload.description: MTA DDCR established zero percent DBE goal for the Rail Grinding Services contract

payload.claim_text: None available. Currently, the Agencies do not possess the resources to perform the services associated with this contract. Additionally, all three agencies require this work, as Rail Grinding is a vital safety requirement to stop and reduce deformation due to use and friction on the railroad tracks by removing deformations and corrosion on the railway.
payload.description: Statement that no alternatives are available for performing rail grinding services in-house

issues: object_local_observation_id references missing local observation id "entity_meeting_doc_115191_mta_procurement_ops". Submit an observation with local_observation_id "entity_meeting_doc_115191_mta_procurement_ops" before submitting this relation.
